# Apple Silicon (M-series Mac) extras — MLX, an optional alternative to PyTorch
# =============================================================================
# MLX is Apple's array/ML framework. On Apple Silicon it runs on the GPU via
# Metal with unified memory, and is often a faster alternative to PyTorch-MPS
# for training small CNNs from scratch — handy for the Day-4 deep-learning
# practical. YOLO and SAM stay on PyTorch (which uses the MPS backend on Apple
# Silicon), so MLX is an *alternative for the CNN-training portion*, not a
# drop-in replacement for the whole stack.
#
# Requirements / caveats:
#   - Apple Silicon ONLY. There are no MLX wheels for Intel Macs; on Intel Mac,
#     Linux, or Windows this file does nothing useful — use PyTorch instead.
#   - Install it INTO your already-created environment, natively (not in Docker):
#
#       conda activate phenotyping            # or: source .venv/bin/activate
#       pip install -r env/requirements-mac-mlx.txt
#
#     or let the installer detect your Mac and do it for you:
#
#       ./setup.sh --with-mlx
#
#   - Docker on Mac runs a Linux VM with NO Metal/GPU access, so MLX there is
#     CPU-only and pointless for acceleration. For MPS/MLX speedups on Apple
#     Silicon, use the native conda/venv install, not the container.

# Apple's array framework (Metal-accelerated on Apple Silicon)
mlx>=0.31

# Efficient data loading/augmentation pipelines for MLX (optional but handy)
mlx-data>=0.2
